Name: GUIDED
LongName: Guidance for reporting intervention development studies in health research (GUIDED)
Description: GUIDED is a 14-item reporting checklist for intervention development studies in health research, aimed at improving transparency, consistency, and learning from intervention-development processes.
Aim: To improve quality and consistency of reporting of health intervention development studies by covering context, purpose, target population, development approaches, evidence and theory use, stakeholder input, adaptation, uncertainty, and open accessibility.

Scope.Coverage: Health and healthcare manuscripts reporting how interventions were developed before or alongside feasibility/evaluation, including evidence synthesis, theory use, co-design or stakeholder-informed approaches.
Scope.Focus: Transparent reporting of development rationale, methods, evidence inputs, theoretical basis, stakeholder roles, design changes over time, subgroup considerations, residual uncertainties, and accessibility of the development report.
Scope.ContrastWith: GUIDED focuses on intervention development process reporting, not full trial-result reporting. It complements design-specific guidelines (for example CONSORT) and intervention-description guidance (for example TIDieR).

primary.description: Duncan E, O'Cathain A, Rousseau N, et al. Guidance for reporting intervention development studies in health research (GUIDED): an evidence-based consensus study. BMJ Open. 2020;10:e033516.

secondary.description: Open-access mirror of the GUIDED BMJ Open article PDF.

items.item_question: Is the context for which the intervention was developed reported?
items.item_question_background: Context reporting supports transferability judgments across settings.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the setting/context and contextual constraints/opportunities shaping development decisions.

source_page_refs.quote: Item 1 Description: Report the context for which the intervention was developed.

items.item_question: Is the purpose of the intervention development process reported?
items.item_question_background: Purpose clarifies intended problem-solution goals for development work.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract explicit development purpose/objective and intended contribution of the intervention.

source_page_refs.quote: Item 2 Description: Report the purpose of the intervention development...

items.item_question: Is the target population for the intervention reported?
items.item_question_background: Target-population clarity is essential for relevance and implementation planning.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract intended population(s), key characteristics, and inclusion rationale used during development.

source_page_refs.quote: Item 3 Description: Report the target population for the intervention...

items.item_question: Is it reported how any published intervention development approach contributed to the process?
items.item_question_background: Named approaches improve methodological interpretability and reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any formal development frameworks/methods used and how they shaped decisions.

source_page_refs.quote: Report how any published intervention development approach contributed to the development process.

items.item_question: Is it reported how evidence from different sources informed intervention development?
items.item_question_background: Evidence provenance supports credibility of development choices.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract evidence types/sources and explicit links between evidence and intervention decisions.

source_page_refs.quote: Report how evidence from different sources informed the intervention development.

items.item_question: Is it reported how/if existing published theory informed intervention development?
items.item_question_background: Theory links mechanism assumptions with design decisions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether theory was used, which theory, and how it informed components or logic.

source_page_refs.quote: Item 6 Description: Report how/if existing published theory informed the intervention development process.

items.item_question: Is any use of components from existing interventions reported?
items.item_question_background: Distinguishing adapted versus novel components improves interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract adopted/adapted components and their original sources.

source_page_refs.quote: Report any use of components from an existing intervention.

items.item_question: Are guiding principles, people, or factors prioritised during decision-making reported?
items.item_question_background: Priority rules clarify trade-offs during development.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract guiding principles and priority-setting factors influencing design choices.

source_page_refs.quote: Report any guiding principles, people or factors which were prioritised when making decisions.

items.item_question: Is stakeholder contribution to the intervention development process reported?
items.item_question_background: Stakeholder roles affect acceptability, feasibility, and relevance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract who contributed (for example patients/public, clinicians, implementers), when, and how contributions influenced decisions.

source_page_refs.quote: Report how stakeholders contributed to the intervention development process.

items.item_question: Are changes in intervention content/format from the start of development reported (including discarded components and reasons)?
items.item_question_background: Change tracking reveals why final interventions differ from initial concepts.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract what changed during development, why changes occurred, and what components were discarded and why.

source_page_refs.quote: item 10 'Report how the intervention changed in content and format from the start...' and ... reasons for discarding intervention components

items.item_question: Are required or likely required intervention changes for subgroups reported?
items.item_question_background: Subgroup adaptation details support equity and implementation planning.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract planned/required adaptations for relevant subgroups and supporting rationale.

source_page_refs.quote: Report any changes to interventions required or likely to be required for subgroups.

items.item_question: Are important uncertainties at the end of the intervention development process reported?
items.item_question_background: Documenting uncertainty supports appropriate next-stage evaluation planning.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract unresolved uncertainties and their implications for feasibility/evaluation/implementation.

source_page_refs.quote: Report important uncertainties at the end of the intervention development process.

items.item_question: Is TIDieR guidance followed when describing the developed intervention?
items.item_question_background: TIDieR linkage improves consistency of final intervention description detail.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether and how TIDieR was used to structure intervention description/reporting.

source_page_refs.quote: Item 13 Description: Follow TIDieR guidance when describing the developed intervention.

items.item_question: Is the intervention development process reported in an open access format?
items.item_question_background: Open accessibility supports cumulative methodological learning and reuse.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract where/how intervention development reporting is openly accessible (journal, report, website).

source_page_refs.quote: Item 14 Description: Report the intervention development process in an open access format.

Aliases: Guidance for reporting intervention development studies
GUIDance for the rEporting of intervention Development
EvidenceExamplesPositive: The study reports context, purpose, target population, and how evidence and theory informed intervention development decisions.
Stakeholder contributions are described alongside iterative changes in intervention content and unresolved uncertainties.
Authors explicitly use GUIDED and link intervention description to TIDieR reporting.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: The manuscript reports trial outcomes only and does not describe how the intervention was developed.
This is a pure software engineering design paper with no health intervention development context.
The article provides intervention results but no account of evidence/theory/stakeholder inputs used during development.
Matching.Explain: Adds an intervention-development reporting overlay when manuscripts describe how health interventions were designed and iteratively refined before or alongside evaluation.
